title: i got 1,000,000 followers on youtube again...

transcript.text: How are you, Jim? What? What is up, YouTube? This is our first video of this kind. This is an experiment vlog because we got something special going on today that I wanted to highlight. One of our channels was planned to cross a million subs during the day today and I was like, it would be super cool to have it like just record it on the vlog and have our day-to-day activity, you know, I got some shooting scheduled, I got my my morning meetings, everything. But, unfortunately, YouTube decided that today it's not going to be the day and uh 1 million subs already happened tonight. It's like 6:00 a.m. in the morning, but I woke up five times to not miss it because one of our channels is actually just about to hit a million followers. I'm about to call Mario, a founder of this channel and my partner. And we're going to do a little 5:00 a.m. party for channel and it's the third channel in a row that is actually crossing 1 million subs, which I'm super excited about. I missed the second one and it happened at about like 2:00 a.m., so I was sleeping, but I was like, no, I'm not missing this one. So, um I'm going to call Mario and we'll see what he thinks because for him as long as I know, this is the first channel that crosses a million subs and this is a very special channel as well. Yo, what's up, man? How are you? >> Well, I'm going crazy. I I slept like 3 hours. >> I had five alarms every hour basically and I like woke up, I checked the number of followers, I'm like doing the math in my head. I'm like, "Can I sleep 1 more hour or not?" So, I ended up it's like, I don't know, 6:00 a.m. right now, but yeah, it's been a crazy night. When we When we were celebrating our work, we thought it's going to happen at 10:00 p.m. I think I told you this story. So, we we gathered together at like 9:00 p.m. And then it slowed down so much during the night that we ended up celebrating at 7:00 a.m. So, we spent like 9 hours on a call waiting because it like slowed down so much. It during the day, we gained like, I don't know, 10,000 followers. Like it was super quick back then at like when we were hitting the million. So, during the day we gained 10,000, and then during the night we gained another like 2,000 or something. So, I was like, "It doesn't make sense." Like it it just slows down so dramatically. Bro, we have one one more follower. We're just about to hit the million. Every minute now. Damn. That looks so sick, actually. >> There we go. >> Oh, bro. I thought there will be some kind of a animation or whatever. >> Yeah, it should actually happen, but it only happened. >> Bro, that's so crazy. Congrats. That was amazing success. I'm so happy. So, I haven't slept much. I had my alarm basically every hour to not miss it. I already had a call with my partner who we're running this channel together. And we already crossed the million subs. We are actually at 1 million 200 subscribers right now, which is sick. The channel is Zach Films. I'm sure that you laughed at our videos previously, and um been like all over TikTok, all over Instagram, all over like it it's everywhere because uh it's super unique uh and uh yeah, it's one of a kind. We still have an exciting day in front of us. We uh we got the shooting at the studio where I'm going to show like 10 lessons that I learned getting this channel to a million subs. Obviously, it's not that gold play button. This one will arrive in like a month to uh to my partner. Uh but uh yeah, got a little shooting. We're going to go and have a breakfast right now. Um if we have time, we're going to race some cars as well today and uh yeah, this is the agenda for today. My goal with this vlog is to show you the real side of running a faceless YouTube portfolio. I haven't seen anyone do that and uh I think that the space is overall crowded with people saying that it's super easy to run those channels. You can make AI run all of your channels for you, which is semi-true, but I want to show the real side of it. I want to show you how my life as someone who runs a faceless YouTube portfolio that now has three YouTube channels with a million subs. Um I want to show you my side and I want to show you that most of the time it's 24/7 grind. Most of the time it is fun, but at the same time it's hard work that you need to put in. Without further ado, let's get to vlog. All right, and uh for those of you who are absolutely new here, who don't know who I am, I've been running faceless YouTube channels for uh a year and a half now and uh the thing that I'm mostly focused on is branded high-quality channels that uh no one else is prepared to do. So, we've built Art of War to 1.6 million followers. Uh this is the first-ever channel that uh I started about 13 14 months ago and since then we figured out that our strength is actually the business side of YouTube the side that nobody else is talking about that nobody else is even prepared to kind of cover in their content because it's very very simple like the most typical advice that you get is go on Upwork find a freelancer and then freaking set up SOPs but nobody even knows what SOPs mean and as someone who comes from a business background I had an agency for 5 years I scaled it we had 13 employees 90k months like all of that I even sold a part of it to a partner investor so I got the business side of experience. As someone who comes from this background I decided that I want a portfolio of channels branded one in the most complicated niches of all. So we do 3D we do 2D animation we do like complicated AI as well and the reason why we're able to do it is because we hire from high ROI markets we hire full-time team members we build real systems not like some [ __ ] SOPs like actual systems content pipelines we have producers we have script writers animators like whatever it is. And this is exactly how we scaled Zik Films Artlist and Wholesome Memes so we got three channels to 1 million subs in under a year because of those systems. So in this vlog I will try to show you the business side of YouTube that no one else is showing you and I hope that you will enjoy it just as much as I'm going to enjoy my breakfast for today. >> I got it booming. Stats going up we all going dumb not stupid. Trying to keep up with me. I told them all of this. >> And I mean the coolest thing about uh this particular channel is that uh it's the one that I would personally call beauty and business because uh it just like the founder of the channel, Mario, right? My partner. And uh I'll tell you a little bit more about the uh the business model and like how we run it and everything. Uh but he's in charge of scripts and ideation and he's such a caring guy for his audience and therefore basically what he does on the platform. For him, it's not about the money and business side of it like it is for me. And that's why this channel is so special. That's why it turned out so freaking awesome. Like we had IShowSpeed reacting to our videos. We had uh so many creators with like millions of followers just talking about Zack Films and uh the humor that we do. And uh Mario actually prepared something incredible that I will probably share with you later in that vlog because uh he's working on uh the uh 1 million follower video with our biggest competitor. He managed to somehow get the approval from this competitor to use his face and uh we're going to post the craziest uh collab ever uh on this channel today because we want to celebrate a million subs. >> Yeah, yeah. It's one of a kind. Let go. >> Yo. >> Yo. >> You naked? >> Uh I'm just not wearing a t-shirt. Uh if you're outside, I don't know if I can I can show it to you. >> Why? I'm >> I'm loading the video on uh in a minute still on YouTube now. >> Okay. Uh I I have my laptop. I can watch it. >> Yeah, please watch it. I want to see your reaction. >> You want to see my reaction? Okay, give one sec. I'll pull up the laptop. >> to see your erection? You want to see his erection? >> This is uh my operator my my videographer. We're We're doing a vlog today, so >> Oh, nice. >> Yeah, this is going to be on a vlog if you don't mind. So, we we kill multiple birds in one stone. >> Yeah, put the volume so so high. >> Okay, okay, okay. So, I'm watching it. Let's go. Bro, with sound design is so much better. >> Yeah. >> Bro, that's like a movie. I'm telling you. >> Bro, this is not even the best part. >> Yeah, let me see. Oh, fast forward. This is raw. Entire story of the channel in one video. >> Oh. It's just a cow. Yes. >> I know that's like that's the best part. Bro, that's insane. Like that's just I I have no idea like some scenes I can imagine myself doing but like some other scenes I'm like it's impossible to make like just just impossible. [ __ ] movie though. That's a movie. >> Man, how many [ __ ] creators do this [ __ ] >> No one. No one in the world do that. Like no one is even prepared to do it. Like no one has the the creative brain to do it. Like that's what I was saying like it's just uh I don't know how you come up with all of this. It's I don't know whether it's drugs or your perfect brain. >> Uh no drugs because in Vietnam it's very legal. >> Yeah, unfortunately. >> I got sent right into jail in Vietnam. >> Yeah. No jail. All right. So uh Mario is hyped. Mario is going to grab a beer because uh finally we finished this video and the reason why this channel even exists is uh his pure creativity and uh uh he is the founder behind it. So how am I involved in uh into all of this? So as I said previously we found out that our hiring and business systems are exactly what kind of brings value to the um to the deal like something that uh nobody else has. So the business model that was that I was using for the last couple of months is that we find those creative founders, someone who is like an absolute genius of what they do. They love ideation. They love scripts. They are faceless Mr. Beast, but they lack this business side of things, the boring side of things. They lack team management skills, they lack hiring skills, they lack systems, they're unorganized. And we can solve all of those problems. So, I build a team and obviously AI-powered systems around one simple thing. I want to be responsible for all of the boring stuff, for all of the boring like parts of actually running a faceless YouTube business. And then we can go ahead and partner with very creative people like Mario and build channels like Zach Films because any successful YouTube channel requires to have two sides. There it requires to have the creative side, scripts, ideas, formats, everything related to creativity, and it also needs the business approach because without the business approach, you can't scale. You can't have millions of views per day, you can't put out videos consistently. And this is what we are in charge of and this is how we've been partnering up with some of the best creators in the faceless space and helping them scale, basically enhancing their productivity and making sure that they can deliver as much content as possible to their viewers. And right now we are um going to the studio. We're going to do that and uh then for the rest of the day I'm basically just going to attend a bunch of meetings and stuff. I like to push my meetings back a little bit and have them later in the day when you know, my my creative brain is already cooked and I can just focus on the meeting. So, yeah, that's kind of the agenda that we have. All right, so got to to studio. We have an hour here. I have to record YouTube video. We'll see if we can do some short form content as well, but uh Let me show you the behind the scenes of my YouTube channel and uh the people behind it as well. Some guys. >> Yep. >> How are you, Jim? >> Lesson number three. Post more at your peak and scale back when views fall. This is something that took me a long time to understand and uh something that uh cost me a 100 million views uh screenshot because uh Art of Work was the channel that at its peak was generating 40 million views per day. And uh back then, I was only posting uh twice. I have a graph here that shows uh first of all, 4 views. That's how much we generated on this channel. And at the beginning, we were posting one video a day uh pretty much until we got to the point where we were generating 150 million views a month. Then, we started posting twice a day and you can see how the channel exploded from there. Uh but it was still up and down. It was still pretty inconsistent. Uh and then, obviously, when the views uh dropped, we had the uh age restriction couple age restrictions in a row. We got the shadow banned for a second and then, obviously, the channel fell uh fell off a little bit and we started posting one a day again. So, the lesson here is that the moment your channel at its peak and is generating crazy amounts of views, you should be posting more because this just shows that there is a demand for this specific type of content on YouTube right now. All right. Finished shooting. Took a little bit longer than expected. Um But it turned out to be a freaking amazing video. So, excited for that. I don't know if the vlog is actually going to come out sooner or not. But anyways, check that out on the channel. A little bit tired by now, honestly. Because yeah, the first vlog been speaking the whole day. But I do have a bunch of calls lined up. So, the plan about what? 4:00 p.m. right now? Yeah, so the plan is uh we're going to wrap up the vlog. I'm going to take all of those calls and meetings that I have to do. And then it's going to be pretty much the end of the day. I do have a bunch of Discord messages as well to to reply to uh with clients. So, going to do that ASAP after we are done with this uh with this shooting. And after I'm back to the to the studio. But yeah, pretty excited how the vlog is going to turn turn out. Turned [ __ ] me. Yeah, you see I'm already losing the language. But uh excited to see your opinion whether or not we should be doing those vlogs a little bit more often, especially when we have something cool going on. Like hitting a million subs. Pretty cool. And with that said, we're going to wrap up the vlog. And I'll see you guys in the next one.
